hero.lead: The European Youth Movement gives a voice to young people who believe in a truly united Europe, built on pragmatism, fairness, and responsibility.

why.label: Why join us
why.title: Why Join Us
why.lead: The only way to build a European future is to take personal responsibility for change — and the new generations have the strength to do it.
why.pillar1_text: Only by knowing one another, acknowledging different realities and sharing a common political journey can we truly feel like citizens of Europe and create the conditions to act together.
why.pillar2_text: Social, cultural and political diversity is not a limitation — it is the fertile ground on which to cultivate dialogue and give rise to shared political positions, capable of combining unity of vision with respect for differences.
why.motto:
Don't think as a voter,
think as a doer!
why.cta_text: United by a pragmatic, positive approach to finding solutions together. Make your contribution and be an active part of change — join the European Youth Movement.

objectives.label: Our strategic plan
objectives.title: Objectives
objectives.lead: We pursue concrete goals to make young Europeans' voices heard at every level of governance.
objectives.o1_text: Recruit members and national coordinators in every EU member state to build a truly pan-European movement.
objectives.o2_text: Produce rigorous position papers and policy briefs on the key EU challenges, submitted to European institutions.
objectives.o3_text: Organise training events, simulations and debates to empower young citizens with political knowledge and skills.
objectives.o4_text: Establish formal dialogue channels with the European Parliament, Commission and other EU bodies.
objectives.o5_text: Grow our communications through social media, a newsletter, articles and media appearances to broaden our reach.
objectives.o6_text: Europe's strength lies in its diversity. By connecting young Europeans across borders — through dialogue, shared experiences and a common political journey — we cultivate a genuine sense of belonging that makes a united Europe not just an institution, but a living community.

newsletter.title: Stay informed
newsletter.lead: Subscribe to our newsletter — open to everyone, members and non-members alike.
newsletter.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

policies_section.label: Working Groups
policies_section.title: Policies
policies_section.lead: Each thematic area is led by a dedicated working group. Join the conversation on Telegram and read the positions our members have developed.
policies_section.papers_placeholder_text: EYM opens its platform to every member who wishes to contribute — with position papers, analytical articles or policy briefs. We are working to build a direct channel with European institutions to give visibility to the media and written production emerging from our community.
policies_section.papers_placeholder_cta: Want to contribute? Join the Movement →
policies_section.papers_remind: Members can submit position papers, articles and policy briefs through their Thematic Group.

wg.fp_desc: EU foreign policy, strategic autonomy, global conflict prevention, transatlantic relations, the Eastern neighbourhood and EU enlargement.
wg.ds_desc: European strategic autonomy, defence cooperation, NATO relations, joint military capabilities and rearmament policies.
wg.ee_desc: European energy portfolio, nuclear energy and renewables, energy inequality, biodiversity and sustainable development in Europe.
wg.jus_desc: Rule of law, fundamental rights, anti-corruption efforts and the strengthening of democratic institutions.
wg.edu_desc: European education policy, youth mobility, Erasmus+, European university hubs, skills development and lifelong learning.
wg.hc_desc: European Health Union, mental health, cross-border access to healthcare and pharmaceutical policy.
wg.ihr_desc: Migration policy, management of irregular migration, refugee rights, integration, social cohesion and the protection of fundamental freedoms.

movement.label: Who we are
movement.title: The Movement
movement.lead: A pan-European association built on a clear organisational structure, a founding statute and a shared code of ethics.
movement.ga_desc: The sovereign body of the movement. All members participate in strategic decisions, statutes and elections.
movement.eb_desc: The governing body responsible for day-to-day leadership, coordination and external representation.
movement.tc_desc: One coordinator per working group, responsible for policy development and member engagement on each topic.
movement.cm_desc: Team responsible for social media, editorial content, the newsletter and external communications strategy.
movement.nc_desc: Representatives based in each EU member state, responsible for member recruitment and local activities.
movement.ld_desc: Responsible for legal oversight, ethics enforcement, financial auditing and the integrity of the movement's governance.
movement.statute_desc: The founding statute governs the life of the association, its objectives, activities and governing bodies.
movement.statute_soon: The statute document will be available for download shortly.
movement.ethics_desc: The code of ethics defines the values and conduct expected of all members — integrity, respect, accountability and the European spirit.

territory.label: Our presence
territory.title: Territory
territory.lead: The European Youth Movement is growing across Europe. The map below will show registered members by country as our network expands.
territory.map_desc: Hover over a country to see its name. Countries highlighted in blue have registered EYM members.
territory.nc_cta: We are looking for national coordinators in every EU member state. Interested in representing EYM in your country?

events.label: What's on
events.title: Events
events.lead: Conferences, debates, training sessions and more — all our upcoming events in one place.
events.soon_text: Our events calendar is being set up. Check back shortly or subscribe to the newsletter to be notified first.

news.label: Press & Media
news.title: News
news.lead: Articles from external contributors about the movement, interviews and media appearances.
news.n1_title: A Space for Press & Media Coverage
news.n1_text: EYM is building a dedicated press area to collect external articles, mentions and statements about the movement — a transparent window on how European media covers our work.
news.n2_text: Here we will publish interviews with EYM representatives — in print, podcast and video — where members and leadership share the movement's ideas with a wider audience.
news.n3_text: Our YouTube channel will host recordings of events, working group sessions and debates — making EYM's contribution to European public discourse accessible to all.
news.ig_text: Updates, events and behind-the-scenes content from the European Youth Movement community.

footer.brand_desc: A pan-European movement expanding its network across Europe, publishing policy proposals, fostering political education and building direct dialogue with EU institutions — to forge a shared European identity together.
footer.copyright: © 2026 European Youth Movement. All rights reserved.
footer.motto: ★ For a united and democratic Europe ★

modals.fp_body:
The Foreign Policy working group addresses EU foreign policy, strategic autonomy, global conflict prevention, transatlantic relations, the Eastern neighbourhood and EU enlargement.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.ds_body:
The Defence & Security working group addresses European strategic autonomy, defence cooperation, NATO relations, joint military capabilities and rearmament policies.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.ee_body:
The Energy & Environment working group addresses the European energy portfolio, nuclear energy and renewables, energy inequality, biodiversity and sustainable development in Europe.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.jus_body:
The Justice working group addresses the rule of law, fundamental rights, anti-corruption efforts and the strengthening of democratic institutions across Europe.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.edu_body:
The Education working group addresses European education policy, youth mobility, Erasmus+, European university hubs, skills development and lifelong learning.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.hc_body:
The Healthcare working group addresses the European Health Union, mental health, cross-border access to healthcare and pharmaceutical policy.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.ihr_body:
The Immigration & Human Rights working group addresses migration policy, the management of irregular migration, refugee rights, integration, social cohesion and the protection of fundamental freedoms.

Our vision for Europe

Our ideological line takes shape through open debate within the movement's official channels. Help build our position — Join the Working Group →

Position Papers and detailed proposals from this working group are published in the Policies section.
modals.ga_body:
The General Assembly is the sovereign deliberative body of the European Youth Movement. It brings together all members to decide on the movement's strategy, statute and leadership.

Member profiles and bios will be published here as the assembly is constituted.
modals.ga_member_bio: Bio and contact details coming soon.
modals.eb_body: The Executive Board leads the day-to-day activities of EYM, coordinates working groups and represents the movement externally.
modals.tc_body: Each working group is led by a thematic coordinator responsible for policy research, member engagement and position paper publication.
modals.cd_body: The Communication & Marketing department manages EYM's public presence, social media strategy, editorial content and newsletter.
modals.nc_body:
National coordinators are EYM's local representatives in each EU member state. They recruit members, organise local activities and liaise with the Executive Board.

If your country is not yet represented, apply to become a coordinator.
modals.ld_body:
The Legal Department is responsible for legal oversight, ethics enforcement, financial auditing and ensuring the integrity of the movement's governance structures.

Member profiles and details will be published here.

signup.page_title: Join the Movement — European Youth Movement
signup.form_lead: Complete the form below to become a member. We'll review your application and be in touch shortly.
signup.wg_hint: You can join more working groups after registration.
signup.bio_placeholder: Tell us about your background and what draws you to this project...
signup.newsletter_label: I'd like to receive the EYM newsletter
signup.ethics_html: I have read and accept the Code of Ethics and the Founding Manifesto of the European Youth Movement.
signup.success_text: Your membership request has been received. You will hear from us shortly with next steps and access to your working group.

apply.page_title: Apply as National Coordinator — European Youth Movement
apply.form_lead: Fill in the form below to apply as a national coordinator for the European Youth Movement in your country.
apply.info_title: What does a National Coordinator do?
apply.info_text: National Coordinators are EYM's local representatives. They recruit members, organise activities in their country, and act as a bridge between their national community and the EYM Executive Board.
apply.motivation_placeholder: Tell us why you want to become a national coordinator and what you would bring to the role...
apply.success_text: Thank you for applying as a National Coordinator. We'll review your application and get back to you soon.
